data.title: Bank OZK GAAP EPS of $1.55 beats by $0.01, revenue of $423M in-line
data.description:
• Bank OZK press release (NASDAQ:OZK): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.55 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $423M (+7.6% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Loans were $29.22 billion at September 30, 2024, a 15.3% increase from $25.33 billion at September 30, 2023.
  • Deposits were $30.57 billion at September 30, 2024, a 19.6% increase from $25.55 billion at September 30, 2023.
  • Book value per common share was $46.31 at September 30, 2024, a $5.96 increase from $40.35 at September 30, 2023.

data.title: Fresh2 Group receives delisting letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Fresh2 Group (NASDAQ:FRES) said on Thursday that on October 16, 2024, it received a letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company did not comply with Nasdaq’s filing requirements.
  • Nasdaq had previously granted the company an exception until September 27, 2024, to regain compliance.
  • Nasdaq has determined that the company has not met the terms of the exception, as the company has yet to file the necessary reports.
  • Fresh2 intends to request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the delisting determination, to stay the suspension of the Company’s securities for 15 days from the date of the hearing request. The Company also plans to request a stay of the suspension pending the hearing.

data.title: Dow notches 39th record close of 2024, while Nasdaq ekes out gains; S&P slips marginally
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday scaled a fresh all-time intraday high on a boost from technology stocks and a robust retail sales report that bolstered expectations of a soft landing, but then gave up those gains in the afternoon and eventually ended largely flat.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) eked out its 39th record close of the year, settling +0.37% at 43,239.05 points. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) also hit a new all-time intraday peak at 5,878.46 points, but pulled back to conclude -0.02% at 5,841.47 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished just above the flat line, +0.04% at 18,373.61 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, six closed in the red.

Technology added +0.4%, getting a boost from chip stocks. Disappointing quarterly results and guidance from Dutch computer chip equipment supplier ASML (ASML) two days ago dragged down the sector. However, a blowout report earlier today from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), the world's largest contract chipmaker, reinvigorated the artificial intelligence (AI) trade.

Additionally, a post-earnings +9% pop in Dow 30 component Travelers (TRV) provided a lift to the S&P 500 (SP500). The insurer comfortably beat quarterly expectations on a robust underwriting and investment income performance.

Conversely, Elevance Health (ELV) slipped -10.6%. The health insurer slashed its full-year adjusted profit guidance on rising medical costs, a development that also dragged down managed care peers such as Molina Healthcare (MOH) -12.6%, and Centene (CNC) -9.1%.

All eyes will be on Netflix (NFLX), with the streaming leader scheduled to announce earnings after the closing bell.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were higher. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was up 8 basis points to 4.09%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 3.98%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

The climb in yields came as investors dumped bonds following stronger-than-anticipated September retail sales. The headline figure grew 0.4% M/M from August's +0.1% reading, while topping the consensus of +0.3%. The data showed that the U.S. consumer was chugging along, strengthening expectations that the Federal Reserve would be able to deliver a soft landing.

"This is the kind of market where both bears and bulls find good arguments to strengthen their theses. On the one hand, economic data is good. Retail data came in strong, job data earlier this month was healthy, and the economic surprise index reached its highest level since April today," Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha.

"On the other hand, there is no way the market can price in two more rate cuts this year. Especially if inflation remains above the Fed’s target, we may have to ask ourselves what it could mean for the market if rates remain elevated a bit longer than expected. As the market trades at 22x forward earnings, we somewhat rely on the Fed to achieve a soft/no landing," Nelissen added.

Also on Thursday's economic calendar, the number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week fell to 241K.

Meanwhile, monetary policy actions from across the Atlantic grabbed some eyeballs. The European Central Bank delivered a second straight cut in its key policy rate and said that a "disinflationary process" was well on track.

data.title: Netflix GAAP EPS of $5.40 beats by $0.28, revenue of $9.82B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Netflix press release (NASDAQ:NFLX): Q3 GAAP EPS of $5.40 beats by $0.28.
  • Revenue of $9.82B (+15.0% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Global Streaming Paid Memberships +5.07M to 282.72M
  • For 2024, we expect revenue growth of 15% (the high end of our 14% to 15% range), and operating margin of 27% (vs 26% previously).
  • We continue to build our advertising business and improve our offering for advertisers. Ads membership was up 35% quarter on quarter, and our ad tech platform is on track to launch in Canada in Q4 and more broadly in 2025.
  • As we look ahead to 2025, we’re focused on improving every aspect of our service and continuing to deliver healthy revenue and profit growth.
  • Shares +1.64%.

data.title: Curanex, botanical drug developer, files for $10M IPO
data.description:
• Curanex Pharmaceuticals (CURX) has filed for a proposed $10M initial public offering.
  • According to an SEC filing, Curanex is looking to offer 2M shares priced between $4 and $6 per share, which would raise $10M if priced at the midpoint. The company is hoping to list its shares on Nasdaq under the symbol CURX.
  • Dominari Securities and Revere Securities are serving as lead bookrunners on the deal.
  • Based in New York, Curanex’s lead drug candidate, Phyto-N, is derived from a traditional Chinese medicinal plant. The company hopes to file an Investigational New Drug application with the FDA in 2025 to begin clinical testing the product for the treatment of ulcerative colitis.

data.title: World’s largest ETF SPY hits historic milestone as it crosses $600B in AUM
data.description:
The world’s largest exchange-traded fund, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY), hit an historic milestone as it became the world’s first ETF to reach $600B in assets under management.

As of October 17, SPY's AUM totaled $603B.

SPY’s influence on the investment landscape is evident in its impressive trading statistics. The ETF trades 2.7 times more than Apple (AAPL), 3.5 times more than Microsoft (MSFT), and 7.6 times more than Google (GOOG), showcasing its unparalleled level of liquidity.

Moreover, SPY trades more than the next three most liquid ETFs combined, based on year-to-date volume, and the fund accounts for roughly 20% of the entire U.S. ETF trading volume. SPY’s presence on U.S. exchanges are also very significant, as it represents approximately 6% of all exchange volume.

These figures solidify SPY’s status as a cornerstone of global financial markets. As investors continue to flock to low-cost, diversified ETFs, SPY's remarkable growth signals a shift toward passive investing and its increasing role in shaping modern portfolios.

This achievement of surpassing $600B showcases the profound impact SPY has had since its launch as the first U.S.-listed exchange-traded fund, transforming how traders and investors access the market.

Year-to-date performance for SPY: +22.5%.

For traders looking to track SPY and its accompanying benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500), here are some other ETFs and mutual funds that may be of interest:(VOO), (IVV), (SSO), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), and (VFFSX).

data.title: Janet Yellen warns Trump's proposed tariffs would reignite inflation
data.description:
Former President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs would reignite inflation and negatively impact the U.S. economy if he were to reclaim the presidency, Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen cautioned in a Thursday speech.

Trump is proposing a blanket 10% tariff on all imported goods entering the U.S. from abroad, alongside imposing tariffs of 60% or higher on imports from China. Such broad-based levies would be inflationary, economists have suggested, on the premise that U.S. consumers would pay for them rather than foreign governments.

Although Yellen did not explicitly mention Trump, she outlined the risks associated with "sweeping, untargeted tariffs," including an increase in "prices for American families," as well as stifling business competition, according to remarks prepared for delivery at a Council of Foreign Relations conference.

"Calls for walling America off with high tariffs on friends and competitors alike, or by treating even our closest allies as transactional partners are deeply misguided," Yellen said.

A Wall Street Journal survey showed a majority of economists think Trump's proposed policies will boost the U.S. government's deficit and increase inflation and interest rates by more than those of Vice President Kamala Harris.

Elsewhere in her remarks, the article said, Yellen underscored how "we need to promote policies, investments and institutions that support global growth, protect financial stability and avoid economic instability."

She touted the U.S.'s "historic economic recovery" from the pandemic era, saying "GDP growth is strong, our unemployment rate is near historic lows, and inflation has declined significantly." Yellen added that productivity growth has been strong, and "more prime-age Americans are participating in our labor force than at any point over the past two decades."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
